subjects.subject: SocialStudies

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Year End Celebration: Favourite Books and Reading Reflections

subjects.subject: Science

options: Smaller animals like fish and rabbits

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Grade 3 Social Studies Celebration: Our Community, Our World

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Advertising and Persuasion
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Media Literacy strand: advertisements are created to persuade people to buy or believe something, often using bright colours, catchy slogans, and famous people or characters.

quiz.q: An advertisement is mainly created to ___.
options: Persuade people to buy or believe something

quiz.q: Which is a common technique used in ads?

options: To make the cereal taste different
To catch children's attention and make it appealing

quiz.q: A slogan is ___.
options: A short, catchy phrase that sticks in your memory

quiz.q: Being a smart media viewer means ___.
options: Thinking critically about what an ad wants you to do

subjects.subject: Math

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Number strand: rounding means changing a number to the nearest ten or hundred to make it easier to work with, based on whether the next digit is 5 or more.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Growth and Changes in Plants: The Life Cycle
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Science Life Systems strand: plants grow through a life cycle -- seed, germination, seedling, mature plant, flower, and new seeds -- repeating the pattern.

quiz.q: What is the first stage of a plant's life cycle?

options: A seed beginning to sprout and grow

quiz.q: After a seedling grows, it becomes a ___.

options: A straight line that never repeats
The same as an animal's life cycle
A repeating pattern from seed to seed

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Physical Regions of Canada: The Canadian Shield
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the Canadian Shield is a huge region of ancient rock, forests, and lakes that covers much of central and northern Canada, including most of Ontario.

quiz.q: The Canadian Shield is mostly made of ___.

options: People dug them all by hand
The rock holds water well, and ancient glaciers carved out basins

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Making Connections
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Reading strand: strong readers make connections between a text and themselves (text-to-self), other texts (text-to-text), or the wider world (text-to-world) to deepen understanding.

quiz.q: A text-to-self connection means the reader ___.
options: Compares the text to a news event
Compares the text to another book
Relates the text to their own life or experiences

quiz.q: A text-to-world connection means relating a text to ___.
options: Events or issues happening in the wider world

quiz.q: Why do readers make connections while reading?
options: To better understand and remember the text

quiz.q: A text-to-text connection compares ___.
options: A book and the reader's shoes

quiz.q: If a story about moving to a new school reminds you of your own first day, that is a ___ connection.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing and Ordering 3-Digit Numbers
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Number strand: students compare 3-digit numbers by looking at the hundreds digit first, then tens, then ones, and order sets of numbers from least to greatest.

Which symbol means greater than?
Which is smallest: 205, 250, or 502?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Growth and Changes in Plants: Pollination and Seed Dispersal
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Science Life Systems strand: pollination moves pollen from flower to flower (often by insects like bees), and seed dispersal spreads seeds by wind, animals, or water so new plants can grow in new places.

quiz.q: Which insect commonly helps pollinate flowers?
Pollination is the process of ___.
options: Moving pollen between flowers to help make seeds

quiz.q: Which is a way seeds can be dispersed?
options: Carried by wind, animals, or water
They never move from the flower

quiz.q: Why is seed dispersal important?
options: It lets new plants grow in new areas instead of competing with the parent plant
It stops plants from growing at all

quiz.q: A dandelion seed with a fluffy parachute is adapted for dispersal by ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Physical Regions of Canada: The Prairies
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the Prairies are a large, flat region in central Canada known for rich farmland, wheat and canola fields, and wide-open grasslands.

quiz.q: The Prairie region is known for being ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Subject-Verb Agreement
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Writing strand: the subject and verb in a sentence must agree in number -- singular subjects take singular verbs (the dog runs) and plural subjects take plural verbs (the dogs run).

quiz.q: Which sentence has correct subject-verb agreement?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Measurement: Mass (Grams and Kilograms)
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Measurement strand: mass is measured in grams (g) for lighter objects and kilograms (kg) for heavier objects, using a balance scale to compare.

Which unit would best measure the mass of a large dog?

subjects.subject: Science

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: a strong structure resists breaking under a load, while a stable structure resists tipping over; shape (like triangles) and a wide base help both.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Physical Regions of Canada: The Rocky Mountains

Which province contains most of the Canadian Rockies?

options: For scenic beauty, hiking, and wildlife viewing
There is nothing to see there

quiz.q: Which animal might you expect to see in the Rocky Mountains?

subjects.subject: Language

options: Use vivid sensory details to help readers picture the scene
State facts plainly with no detail

options: The five senses: sight, sound, smell, touch, taste

options: To help the reader imagine the scene vividly

subjects.subject: Math

options: How much a container can hold

subjects.subject: Science

To make the structure look different

options: Adding extra support where it was weak
Making it taller with no other changes

options: Much less effort for basic needs

subjects.subject: Math

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Communities in Canada, 1780-1850: Transportation and Travel

options: By foot, horse, canoe, or boat

options: They were used for trade and transportation

quiz.q: Which vehicle would NOT have existed in 1780-1850?

subjects.subject: Language

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Writing strand: persuasive writing tries to convince the reader to agree with an opinion or take an action, using reasons and evidence to support the writer's point of view.

options: Convince the reader to agree with an opinion or take action

quiz.q: A strong persuasive piece includes ___.
options: Clear reasons and evidence supporting the opinion

options: Everyone should recycle because it protects our planet.

options: A joke unrelated to the topic

options: To convince others to support a cause or idea

subjects.subject: Math

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Number strand: estimating means using rounded numbers to quickly find an approximate answer to an addition or subtraction problem, useful for checking if an exact answer makes sense.

quiz.q: Estimate: 198 + 302 is approximately ___.

options: Quickly check if an exact answer is reasonable
Get the exact answer with a calculator

subjects.subject: Science

options: Earth is tilted as it orbits the Sun

quiz.q: One full orbit of Earth around the Sun takes about ___.
It is daytime on the side of Earth that ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies

options: A statement that can be measured exactly

subjects.subject: Math

subjects.subject: SocialStudies

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: comparing tools and technology from 1780-1850 to today shows how communication, farming, and travel have changed dramatically over time.

quiz.q: Comparing then and now helps us understand ___.

That change happens over time due to new inventions

subjects.subject: Language

subjects.subject: Math

A single object cut into pieces only

subjects.subject: SocialStudies

quiz.q: How many territories does Canada have?

subjects.subject: Language

options: Share information or stories with readers

subjects.subject: Math

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 3 Science strand connecting to Structures and Matter: conservation means using resources wisely to protect the environment, and recycling turns used materials into new products instead of sending them to landfill.

quiz.q: Conservation means ___.
options: Using resources wisely to protect the environment
Using as much as possible with no limits

options: Turning used materials into new products instead of waste

options: Turning old paper into new paper products
Throwing a plastic bottle in the garbage

options: Broken electronics thrown in a lake

options: It helps protect resources so they last for future generations
